title: Fix a typo in batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap
body:
The current implementation can cause `reproj_mask` to be undefined in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap()` if `max_reproj_error = None` causing the following: 

```
if masks is not None and reproj_mask is not None:
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'reproj_mask' referenced before assignment
``` 

Initializing `reproj_mask` beforehand fix that error.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a crash in `batch_np_matrix_to_pycolmap` by ensuring `reproj_mask` is always defined when `max_reproj_error` is `None`, preventing an `UnboundLocalError` during mask merging.

steps.operation: Initialize `reproj_mask` to a safe default before the `max_reproj_error` conditional and keep the existing mask-combination semantics intact
steps.reason: `reproj_mask` is currently only assigned inside `if max_reproj_error is not None:` but is referenced afterward unconditionally, causing an `UnboundLocalError` when `max_reproj_error=None`. Setting `reproj_mask = None` before the conditional (or equivalent restructuring) guarantees the variable is always defined and preserves intended behavior when reprojection filtering is disabled.
steps.side_effects: Eliminates the runtime error path for `max_reproj_error=None`. Behavior should remain unchanged otherwise: when `max_reproj_error` is provided, reprojection masking still applies; when it is not, only user-provided `masks` (if any) are used. Note that the existing `assert masks is not None` will still fail if both `masks` and `max_reproj_error` are `None`—this is pre-existing behavior and should be verified against callers/tests.
